content: Is your issue related to Thunderbird desktop or Thunderbird on Android?
creator: platform34

content: This ticket is a duplicate of Kevin's question on https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1561482
creator: platform34

content:
If your question relates to Thunderbird Android,

  1. Open the settings page.
  2. Select your gmail account.
  3. Press the 3 dot menu, top right on the page.
  4. Press remove account.

You'll receive desktop specific support on their forum linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/thunderbird

I'm not familiar with the desktop version, AI answer's as follows.

Steps to remove an account Open Thunderbird and go to Account Settings:

  1. Use the menu (≡) > Settings > Account Settings, or look under Tools/Edit > Account Settings depending on your version.
  2. ​In the left pane, click the email account you want to remove (click the top line with the email address, not just its subfolders).
  3. ​At the bottom of the Account Settings window, click Account Actions and choose Remove Account (or Delete in some versions).
  4. ​In the confirmation dialog, choose whether to remove only the account settings or also delete message data stored on your computer, then click Remove/OK to confirm.
  5. ​Close the dialog and restart Thunderbird if prompted or if the account list does not refresh immediately.
